quotes.verbatim_quote:
Approach chords are where you play a chromatic chord one fret above or below
the target chord you’re aiming for.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This is the foundational definition of the chapter's core technique, establishing the precise mechanical rule for what constitutes an approach chord.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Approach chords bring a tension and release sound to your comping, jazzing up
your chords along the way.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This explains the harmonic purpose of the technique, teaching the player why approach chords work musically, not just how to execute them.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Start with the approach chords from above, then from below, then mix both
together to take your comping to the next level today.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This gives the pedagogical sequence — above, below, combined — which is the structural rule governing how the student should practice and internalize the technique.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
In jazz, you normally see the simplest chord symbol
written on a lead sheet, then you as the performer color that chord from there. So,
it’s good to get used to seeing Cmaj7 but playing the Cmaj9 color as it imitates a
real-life musical situation.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This defines a core jazz performance convention — reading simplified symbols and adding color — that governs how players interpret lead sheets throughout the genre.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
The first exercise features approach chords from above, where you play the same
shape one fret higher, then resolve into your target chord from there.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This specifies the exact mechanical procedure for above-approach chords: same voicing shape, one fret up, then resolve — the actionable rule the player executes on the fretboard.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
You can also work approach chords from below and resolve into your target chord
from there in your comping.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This establishes the second approach direction, completing the two-sided chromatic approach system introduced in the chapter.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
When ready, mix this pattern with the approach chords from above to double
your fun with this workout.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This instructs the student to combine both approach directions, which is the synthesis step that makes the technique practically applicable in real comping.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
feel free to change the rhythms and picking patterns when comfortable to
expand this approach chord exercise even further.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This is an explicit instructional rule that rhythmic and picking variation should be applied once the shapes are secure, teaching the student how to self-extend the exercise.
